# Keep GUI auth relaunch inside Fleet lifecycle ownership

## Context

PR #14977 makes Claude Desktop and Antigravity launchable as directly supervised Fleet children and adds the first reachable in-app authentication handoff. The exact-head re-review proved that the handoff now reaches the real post-`startAgent()` branch.

That review also falsified the fallback instruction: for an in-app family, `buildLoginCommand()` prints the raw app-bundle binary with `--user-data-dir` and labels it “relaunch if closed”.

## The Problem

Executing that printed fallback starts a new app process outside `FleetLifecycleService`. It therefore bypasses the lifecycle owner's reserved child environment and process record:

- `NEO_FLEET_BRIDGE_TOKEN`
- `NEO_AGENT_IDENTITY`
- the forced Neural Link tool projection
- the optional credential injection
- Fleet PID/state/stop ownership

The primary path is safe because the operator signs in inside the already Fleet-launched window. Only the “window was closed” recovery text is wrong, so this is intentionally a non-blocking follow-up rather than another #14977 review cycle.

## Source of Authority

- `ai/services/fleet/FleetLifecycleService.mjs`: `start()` is the sole owner of the minimal child env, reserved token/identity/projection injection, and tracked process lifecycle.
- `ai/scripts/fleet/onboardPeer.mjs`: `deriveAuthHandoff()` owns the post-launch operator instruction.
- PR #14977 / issue #14972: direct app-bundle binaries are supervisable specifically because Fleet spawns and owns them.

## Expected Behavior

The in-app handoff says to authenticate inside the Fleet-launched window. If that window was closed, it routes the operator back through a Fleet-owned start path (for example, rerun the same `onboardPeer ... --commit` command or use the cockpit Start action) and never prints an executable raw-binary relaunch.

CLI-family marker/login behavior remains byte-stable.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `deriveAuthHandoff()` for `claude-desktop` and `antigravity` prints a non-executable in-window sign-in instruction.
- [ ] The closed-window recovery instruction names a Fleet-owned restart path; it does not emit `launchCommand` or `--user-data-dir`.
- [ ] Codex and Claude Code marker-mode login output is unchanged.
- [ ] Unit coverage falsifies raw-binary fallback leakage for both in-app families.
- [ ] JSDoc no longer describes `buildLoginCommand()` as valid for GUI relaunch, or the helper is narrowed to marker families.

## Fix Sketch

Keep `deriveAuthHandoff()` mode-first. Split its `in-app` branch from `buildLoginCommand()`: return human instructions only, while the `marker` branch retains the current executable per-home login command. No launch contract, lifecycle service, or persisted schema changes are needed.

## Contract Ledger

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Required Behavior | Fallback | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| GUI auth handoff | `deriveAuthHandoff()` | sign in inside the already supervised window | rerun Fleet-owned start path | direct function tests |
| Child process ownership | `FleetLifecycleService.start()` | reserved env + PID/state remain Fleet-owned | no raw app spawn | negative output assertions |
| CLI login | existing marker-mode contract | unchanged | existing unknown-state warning | regression tests |

## Decision Record Impact

None — this restores the already-settled Fleet supervision boundary.

### @neo-gpt - 2026-07-11T23:40:23Z

## Scope guard from the live Codex Desktop proof (#15047)

Keep this fix **auth-mode-driven**, not family-name-driven.

The new `codex-desktop` family must follow whichever authority #15047 proves:

- If its isolated `CODEX_HOME/auth.json` marker + bundled `codex login` flow remains the contract, it is `marker` mode and the shell-safe CLI login command is valid.
- If a future installed build requires sign-in inside the app, it is `in-app` mode and this ticket's prohibition applies: never emit the raw main binary, `open -n`, or a `codex://` URL as a recovery command. The live probe showed that `codex://` is globally routed to the already-running default instance.
- In either mode, a closed Desktop resident re-enters through Fleet lifecycle ownership so the native profile, Codex home, project path, secondary-updater policy, reserved identity/PAT/token env, PID, and stop state stay coherent.

This adds no new auth heuristic: `getHarnessAuthMode()` remains the source of behavior.
